sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven has struggled recently, with only 1 win in their last 5 matches and a poor defensive record, conceding 12 goals. Standard Liège has had mixed results, managing 2 wins but suffering heavy defeats, including a 3-0 loss in their last two away games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Oud-Heverlee Leuven has been unbeaten, with a 1-1 draw and a 1-0 win against Standard Liège. Matches between these teams tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in most recent encounters.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Leuven's defensive frailties are evident, ranking last in goals conceded (12), while Standard Liège has shown inconsistency in attack and defense. Both teams average around 5.2 corners per game, indicating potential for set-piece opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Leuven's home advantage and Standard Liège's poor away form could play a role. Additionally, Leuven's tendency to score late (76-90 minutes) might be crucial if the game remains tight.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Overall half-time summary
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline remains goalless between Oud-Heverlee Leuven and Standard Liège at The King Power AT Den Dreef Stadium. Both teams have shown intent but lacked the clinical edge to break the deadlock. The game has been tightly contested with a fairly even split in possession, though Standard Liège has held a slight advantage in controlling the ball. Neither side has managed to convert their entries into the final third into clear scoring opportunities, resulting in a stalemate after the first 45 minutes.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven have shown more presence in the attacking areas with a higher number of penalty area entries compared to Standard Liège. Their duel-winning ability has been slightly better, indicating a more physical and competitive approach in midfield battles. On the other hand, Standard Liège have been marginally more dominant in possession and have secured more corner kicks, suggesting they are looking to create chances from set-pieces. Defensively, both teams have been solid, with neither conceding significant chances in the first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Early on, Oud-Heverlee Leuven took control with consistent attacking spells, pushing forward with intent. However, Standard Liège responded with a strong period of sustained pressure in the middle of the half, dominating for a significant stretch. Towards the latter stages, Leuven regained the upper hand with a series of promising attacking moves, finishing the half on a stronger note. These shifts suggest both teams are capable of taking the initiative in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Oud-Heverlee Leuven, Casper Terho has been a notable threat in attack, managing to get shots on target and showing potential to unlock the opposition defense. Siebe Schrijvers also stands out with his ability to create opportunities in the final third. On Standard Liège's side, Tobias Mohr has shown glimpses of danger with his positioning and involvement in forward play. Casper Nielsen has been influential in midfield, contributing both defensively and in setting up attacks, and could be pivotal in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Disciplinary concerns
halftimeSections.text: Both teams have picked up two yellow cards each in the first half, indicating a physical and intense encounter. For Leuven, Thibault Vlietinck and Takuma Ominami need to be cautious to avoid further bookings that could lead to suspensions or impact their game. Similarly, Standard Liège's Marco Ilaimaharitra and Daan Dierckx will have to tread carefully to maintain their full strength on the pitch. This level of aggression could influence tactical decisions in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see both teams push harder for a breakthrough, given the lack of goals so far. Oud-Heverlee Leuven might capitalize on their stronger presence in the penalty area to create more clear-cut chances. Standard Liège, with their slight edge in possession, could look to control the tempo and exploit set-piece opportunities. With both sides showing attacking intent at different stages, it’s reasonable to expect at least a couple of goals as fatigue sets in and defenses potentially open up.
